coldOpen.text: Welcome. There are two doors. Adventurer means you have time and curiosity. Patron means you have neither — and that's not an insult. He built both versions. Pick whichever you actually are, not which one sounds cooler. He'll know.

returningVisitor.text: Back again. You know the drill.
Back. Either he's hiring you or you're stalling on something at work. Probably both.

pickAdventurer.text: Adventurer. He bet me you'd pick this. He doesn't like losing, so he won.

pickPatron.text: Patron. Practical. The character sheet stays in the drawer for you. You're welcome.

swapView_first.text: Pick a lane. He didn't — he built both. You're welcome. He'd like that weekend back, though.

swapView_second.text: Pick a lane. He didn't get to — he had to build both. You're welcome.

swapView_third.text: You switched. No consequences for you. Usually it costs him a weekend you'll never see.

swapView_fourth.text: Mid-scope flip. He's done this for clients — he shipped them. He'll ship you too.

swapView_repeat.text: Okay, you're doing this on purpose now. He respects it.

clickStat_strength.text: We don't talk about Strength. The character's never invested a single point.

clickStat_caffeine.text: Caffeine Resistance. Negative modifier. He drinks coffee anyway. Calls it a superpower. We disagree.

clickSkill_unity.text: Unity. Cast daily since 2019. Bonus damage vs. spaghetti code. Disadvantage on version-migration days.

clickSkill_unreal.text: Unreal 5. He used this on a DFI-funded vertical slice. Solo programmer. It shipped. He's proud of it. He won't say that.

clickSkill_csharp.text: C#. Six years deep. He has opinions about async. Don't ask unless you have time.

clickFaction_rohan.text: Rohan. Horse lords. He likes that the cool guys ride in late and turn the battle. Says a lot about him.

clickFaction_greyknights.text: Grey Knights. Daemon hunters in silver armor. He paints them slowly. Nine years in, three hundred to go. He'll finish. Probably.

clickFaction_ttrpg.text: That's the system he's building. Don't ask when it's done. Polite answer: 'soon.' Real answer: 'when it's right.'

clickFaction_faerun.text: Faerûn, but his. Six years of edits. The canon doesn't survive contact with his players.

clickLibrary.text: Empty. He doesn't read books. He's mentioned it twice. He's going to mention it again.

clickLibrary_film_beginnersGuide.text: Of course he likes that one. We're basically inside it right now.

clickProject_novo.text: Novo. The day job. Healthcare training simulations. He can't show you most of it. He'll tell you it's the most interesting NDA he's ever signed.

clickProject_khora.text: Khora. The SXSW one. He won't tell you which award, he'll tell you what the team built.

clickProject_silkroad.text: Silkroad. Sole programmer. Unreal 5. A vertical slice that shipped. He was CTO for two years and won't say the word 'CTO' out loud unless you ask him to.

clickProject_ttrpg.text: His TTRPG. Rules. Setting. Stories. Slowly growing. He won't show you everything — some of it isn't ready. The rest he just hasn't decided you've earned yet.

clickProject_3dprint.text: Resin. FDM. Minis, statues, the occasional bracket that fixes something he should have just bought. He had a company for this. He doesn't talk about why it ended. Ask him over coffee.

idle_30s.text: Still reading? Most people skim. Don't tell him you actually read it — he won't believe you.
You're really reading this. He'd want to argue with you. About something. Probably this.

fastSkim.text: Skimming. Bold. He'd respect you more if you slowed down. He's also doing the same thing in another tab right now.

openDevTools.text: You opened the inspector. Looking for the seams. Good. He builds for that kind of audience.

openConsole.text: You found the terminal. He hoped you would. Type 'help' if you're stuck. Type 'cast fireball' if you're not.

konami.text: He hoped someone would try that. Welcome to the back room.

d20_nat20.text: Nat 20. You got lucky. Take it. You only get one roll — he insisted on that.

d20_nat1.text: Nat 1. Embarrassing. The roll stands. He insisted.

d20_normal.text: A respectable roll. Nothing's going to happen because of it. He thought that was funnier.

cursedButton_first.text: There it is. I told him to label it 'do not click' and you'd press it harder. He bet me dinner.

cursedButton_repeat.text: You're really committed to breaking this. Fine. He admires the persistence.

openShowcase_ttrpg.text: He puts the sheet down. Behind it: the manuscript. The good stuff. Or the part he'll let you see.

openShowcase_minis.text: The desk. Paints out of order. Wash drying. He'd apologize for the mess. He won't mean it.

openShowcase_faerun.text: He flips open the DM screen. Six years of notes. Most of them are the players' fault.

openShowcase_khora.text: The XR work. Cleaner room, headset on the desk. The award's in a box somewhere. He moved twice.

closeShowcase.text: Back to the sheet. He picks it up. We continue.

lateNight.text: 3 a.m.? He'd be up too. He'd tell you to sleep, then keep working.

tabBlurred.text: You left. He's not offended. He left tabs open during his own honeymoon.

audioOn.text: Voice on. Hello. You can hear me. That's still novel for both of us.

audioOff.text: Muted. Probably for the best. You'll still see me. Like a subtitle. With opinions.
